events.label: AP Bifurcation — Telangana Separation
events.detail: Telangana separated from Andhra Pradesh on June 2, 2014. Hyderabad + Rangareddy + Medchal RTOs (accounting for ~45–53% of undivided AP registrations) transferred to Telangana. AP Vahan data shows a structural ~50% step-down from this date. THIS IS NOT A DEMAND EVENT. All YoY comparisons across June 2014 in AP data are invalid.

events.label: COVID-19 Lockdown + BS6 Norm Transition
events.detail: COVID-19 national lockdown (Mar 23, 2020) closed all RTOs simultaneously. April 2020 near-zero registrations. Simultaneously, BS6 emission norms took effect April 1, 2020, ending BS4 vehicle sales. March 2020 saw front-loading rush (BS4 sell-off). This is the highest-magnitude anomaly in the entire Vahan dataset. The subsequent months will show artificially high YoY growth due to near-zero base.

events.label: BS6 Mandatory — BS4 Sell-off Peak
events.detail: BS6 emission norms mandatory from April 1, 2020 (Supreme Court order). Dealers and OEMs rushed to clear BS4 inventory in March 2020 — creating a front-load spike. April 2020 registrations then cliffed due to both BS6 transition AND COVID lockdown simultaneously.

events.label: Supreme Court BS3 Ban — 2-Day Notice
events.detail: Supreme Court ordered ban on BS3 vehicle registrations with only 2 days notice (order March 29, effective April 1, 2017). Dealers were left with BS3 inventory they could not register. This creates an OEM-level cliff — particularly visible in Maker-dimension data for OEMs with high BS3 diesel inventory. No demand signal — this is a supply/compliance event.

events.label: BS4 Nationwide + GST Composite Window
events.detail: Apr–Jul 2017 contains three overlapping events: BS4 national rollout (Apr 1), pre-GST buy rush (June — dealers clearing old inventory), and GST launch disruption (July 1). Registration patterns in this 4-month window reflect simultaneous norm transition and tax reform. Any anomaly in this window may be attributed to either or both. Cannot be cleanly separated.

events.label: NBFC Credit Crisis — IL&FS Collapse
events.detail: IL&FS default in September 2018 triggered an NBFC liquidity crisis. Auto financing dried up for 2-wheelers, 3-wheelers, and commercial vehicles. SIAM reports FY2019 vehicle sales declined 15% YoY. Effect was sustained for 6 months (Q3 FY19 through Q4 FY19). AP shows stronger signal due to above-average auto-rickshaw (CNG 3W) density.

events.label: FAME II Launched — ₹10,000 Cr EV Subsidy
events.detail: Cabinet approved FAME II with ₹10,000 Cr allocation for 3 years (April 2019). ₹10,000/kWh subsidy for 4W EVs enabled Tata Nexon EV launch at ₹13.99L. EV 4W registrations tripled in FY20. AP benefits from this plus its own concurrent road tax exemption (A23).

events.label: FAME II Fraud Crackdown — EV 2W Disruption
events.detail: MHI investigation found Okinawa, Hero Electric, Ola Electric and others violated localisation norms and claimed FAME II subsidies fraudulently. Subsidy payments withheld from top-5 e2W OEMs. EV 2W registrations plateaued/dipped May–July 2022. This is a supply-payment disruption, not demand cooling. Recovery once compliance confirmed.

events.label: Russia-Ukraine War — Fuel Price Shock
events.detail: Russia-Ukraine war (Feb 2022) caused global fuel price spike. India CNG price rose from ₹43/kg to ₹80/kg in Delhi (81% increase, Apr–Oct 2022). This temporarily eroded CNG's operating cost advantage. CNG vehicle registration GROWTH RATE halved — not absolute decline. Diesel also suppressed. EV comparatively benefitted. CNG resumed growth as it remained cheaper in total cost of ownership.

events.label: FAME II Ends — Front-load + Cliff
events.detail: FAME II subsidy scheme ended March 2024. Classic front-load: EV 2W registrations elevated in February–March 2024 before expiry. April 2024 cliff as subsidy removed (prices effectively rose ₹1–2L). EMPS 2024 bridged the gap from April 2024 onward.

events.label: COVID-19 Second Wave — State Lockdowns
events.detail: COVID second wave (Delta variant) triggered state-level lockdowns Apr–May 2021. Less severe than 2020 (RTOs partially functional). All segments show 20–30% dip vs trend. AP shows comparable signal to national. Recovery faster than 2020.

events.label: Global Semiconductor Shortage — OEM Supply Cuts
events.detail: Global semiconductor chip shortage hit India OEMs from mid-2021. Maruti suspended production. Waiting periods for popular models extended to 6–12 months. Supply, not demand, was the binding constraint. Registration data understates true demand — retail waitlists were at all-time highs while Vahan registrations were suppressed by supply. Eased from Q4 2022.

events.label: Maruti Suzuki Exits Diesel — Permanent
events.detail: Maruti Suzuki (45%+ of market) decided not to develop BS6-compliant diesel engines, exiting diesel entirely from April 2020. Diesel count for Maruti in Maker-dimension data drops to exactly zero from this date and never recovers. This is a structural, permanent event. Combined with Honda's simultaneous diesel exit, removed ~50% of diesel volume from the market immediately.

events.label: GST 2.0 — Comprehensive Vehicle Rate Rationalisation
events.detail: GST Council's comprehensive rate rationalisation (July 2025) reduced effective GST burden on vehicles. All segments benefitted. Demand stimulation visible in Q2–Q4 2025 registrations. Effect strongest in mid-size segment where the cess reduction was largest.

events.label: Demonetisation — 2W & Rural Demand Dip
events.detail: PM Modi demonetised ₹500/₹1000 notes on Nov 8, 2016. Impact specific to cash-intensive segments: 2-wheelers (₹50k–80k price, often cash purchases) and rural buyers. 4W car registrations (credit/EMI-financed) showed minimal impact. AP rural areas show stronger signal than metro areas. Q4 FY17 2W Petrol dip ~15% in AP.

events.label: Pre-GST Buy Rush + GST Launch Disruption
events.detail: June 2017: dealers cleared old inventory before GST launch — front-load spike especially in Diesel and large-car segments where GST cess increases were steepest. July 2017: dealers recalibrated invoicing under new GST system, causing 2–3 week paralysis. Composite signal — part of the broader BS4/GST Apr–Jul 2017 window.

events.label: BS3 Nationwide Rollout — 2W/3W
events.detail: BS3 emission norms extended to 2-wheelers and 3-wheelers nationwide from April 2010. Front-load spike in March 2010 as dealers cleared BS2 two-wheeler inventory. CNG 3W also affected. Signal mainly in Petrol 2W chart.

events.label: BS4 Nationwide — 2W Front-load
events.detail: BS4 emission norms extended to 2-wheelers nationwide from April 2016. March 2016 shows elevated 2W Petrol registrations (front-load) followed by norm cliff in April 2016. Less severe than 4W transitions due to smaller price differential.

events.label: FAME I Launched — Early EV Stimulation
events.detail: FAME I (Faster Adoption of Manufacturing of EVs) launched April 2015 with ₹895 Cr over 4 years. Signal is small in All India aggregate (EV was <0.1% of market) but visible in EV-specific chart as growth baseline. LOW CONFIDENCE annotation — only relevant for EV fuel-type chart, not visible in Petrol/Diesel views.

events.label: FAME II Subsidy Cut — 40% to 15%
events.detail: MHI reduced FAME II subsidy for 2-wheelers from 40% to 15% of ex-factory price (June 2021). EV 2W prices effectively rose ₹20,000–30,000. Brief demand adjustment dip visible in EV 2W chart. Recovery as OEMs absorbed costs.

events.label: EMPS 2024 — Bridge Subsidy Post-FAME II
events.detail: Electric Mobility Promotion Scheme 2024 launched April 2024 to bridge FAME II expiry gap. Covered EV 2W and 3W with reduced per-vehicle incentive. Prevented a full demand cliff after FAME II ended. Extended to September 2024.

events.label: PM E-DRIVE Scheme — ₹10,900 Cr
events.detail: PM Electric Drive Revolution in Innovative Vehicle Enhancement scheme approved October 2024 with ₹10,900 Cr allocation. Covers EV 2W, 3W, buses with ₹13,000–15,000/kWh subsidy for 4W EVs. Stronger signal than EMPS — larger budget, longer horizon.

events.label: AP EV Policy 2018 — 100% Road Tax Waiver
events.detail: Andhra Pradesh EV Policy 2018–2023 offered 100% road tax exemption for EVs. Road tax on an ₹8L EV can be ₹50,000–80,000 — a material cost reduction. AP EV registrations grew faster than national average from FY2019 onward. Signal strongest in EV 2W and EV 3W (most price-sensitive). AP punches above its income level on EV adoption due to this policy.
sources: AP Govt GO Ms No. 5 Feb 2018

events.label: Mahindra UV Super-cycle — Thar/XUV700/Scorpio N
events.detail: Mahindra launched new-generation Thar (Oct 2020), XUV700 (Oct 2021) and Scorpio N (Jul 2022) — three consecutive blockbuster launches. Mahindra market share jumped from ~5% to ~10%+ in 18 months. Visible as OEM-level spike in Maker-dimension data. Waiting periods of 12–18 months indicate genuine demand exceeding supply.

events.label: Diesel 4W Structural Decline — Post BS6 Premium
events.detail: BS6 raised diesel variant prices by ₹1–2L vs petrol (higher compliance cost). Combined with Maruti and Honda exiting diesel (April 2020), diesel share of PV registrations fell from ~40% in FY18 to ~16% by FY23. This is a multi-year TREND CHANGE, not a single-event anomaly. Annotate as a structural inflection starting FY2021 onwards.

events.label: Strong Hybrid EV Market Birth — Grand Vitara
events.detail: Maruti Suzuki Grand Vitara with Strong Hybrid launched September 2022 alongside Toyota Urban Cruiser Hyryder. First mass-market strong hybrid at ₹15L price point. Strong Hybrid EV category in Vahan data goes from near-zero to material volumes from this month. The +400% YoY growth rate in Oct 2022 is misleading due to near-zero base — absolute volume is what matters.

events.label: Ola Electric S1 Launch — EV 2W Mass Market
events.detail: Ola Electric S1 launched September 2021 at ₹99,999. First mass-market, sub-₹1L electric scooter from a credible brand. EV 2W monthly registrations doubled within 3 months of launch. Triggered competitive response from Hero, TVS, Bajaj — accelerating the entire EV 2W segment.

events.label: COVID Unlock 1.0 — Pent-up Demand Recovery
events.detail: Phased unlock from June 1, 2020. Pent-up demand + COVID-driven preference shift toward personal mobility (away from public transport) drove a sharp V-shaped recovery. June–September 2020 registrations above pre-COVID trend in several segments. Note: high YoY growth in these months is misleading due to near-zero April 2020 base.

events.label: Taxi Aggregator Boom — Ola/Uber Scale-up
events.detail: Ola (2010) and Uber (2013) scaled aggressively 2014–2016. Commercial cab demand drove CNG and Diesel Motor Cab registrations up multi-fold. Effect strong in AP Tier-2 cities (Vizag, Vijayawada) from 2015. Reversed from 2018 as driver incomes collapsed and aggregator growth slowed.

events.label: CNG Price Hike Peak — Growth Rate Halved
events.detail: CNG price rose 81% in 18 months (₹43→₹80/kg in Delhi, Apr 2021–Jun 2022). CNG vehicle new registrations growth rate decelerated — not absolute decline, but rate halved. Part of broader Russia-Ukraine fuel shock cluster. CNG's total cost advantage vs petrol was temporarily compressed. Growth resumed as CNG remained cheaper long-term.

events.label: CNG CGD 9th Round Expansion — National CNG Growth
events.detail: PNGRB 9th City Gas Distribution bidding round (2018) awarded CNG distribution rights to 86 new districts. CNG station network expanded from ~1,400 in 2018 to ~5,000+ by 2022. This supply-side infrastructure expansion directly drove CNG vehicle registration growth — more cities made CNG viable for buyers. AP benefitted late (CNG infrastructure lagged national average in AP).

events.label: Post-COVID Pent-up Demand Surge
events.detail: Post-second wave pent-up demand converged with semiconductor shortage easing (partial) and personal mobility preference shift to drive above-trend registrations Q2 FY22 through FY23. AP rural 2W showed particularly strong recovery. YoY growth rates inflated by low-base effects from Q1 FY21 — absolute volume vs FY20 trend is the correct comparison.

events.label: Semiconductor Shortage Easing — Supply Recovery
events.detail: Global semiconductor shortage eased from Q4 2022. OEM production normalised. Waiting periods for popular models (Creta, Nexon, Brezza) compressed from 12 months to 4–8 weeks. Registration volumes jumped as suppressed backlog orders were fulfilled. This is a supply recovery, not organic demand growth — the underlying demand had been waiting.

events.label: AP Government Change + Amaravati Revival
events.detail: AP state elections May 2024: YSRCP replaced by TDP-led NDA alliance. Government fleet procurement paused during transition. TDP reinstated Amaravati as capital city (Jun 2024) — CV (construction vehicles) and infrastructure demand revival. Temporary Q2 2024 all-segment dip followed by recovery. EV Bus procurement under AP EV Policy accelerated under new government.

events.label: BS6 Phase 2 OBD2 — Diesel HCV Front-load
events.detail: BS6 Phase 2 (OBD2 + RDE requirements) took effect April 2023, primarily affecting heavy commercial vehicles (HCV). Trucking companies and fleet operators front-loaded diesel HCV purchases in Q3/Q4 FY23. Mainly visible in Diesel CV data, minimal impact on passenger vehicle registrations.

events.label: FAME I Ends — Gap Before FAME II
events.detail: FAME I ended March 2019. Brief gap before FAME II (Apr 2019). Minor EV 2W dip visible only in EV-specific chart. Contextual: explains why EV 2W shows minor softness in Feb–Mar 2019 before the FAME II surge.

events.label: Diesel Price Full Deregulation
events.detail: Cabinet fully deregulated diesel prices in October 2014. Diesel price parity with petrol reached by 2015. Diesel's historical 40–60% cost advantage over petrol eroded. Structural context for Diesel's long-term demand decline from 2015 onward — accelerated later by BS6.

events.label: VAHAN 4.0 Platform Migration — Data Quality Event
events.detail: VAHAN 4.0 platform migration in 2017 may have caused methodology changes in how registrations are counted and attributed. Any anomaly in 2017 data should be cross-referenced against this data quality event before attributing to policy causes. SIAM wholesale data is the preferred cross-check for this period.

events.label: Green Tax on Older Vehicles
events.detail: State-level green taxes on older vehicles (>8yr for commercial, >15yr for personal) introduced from 2021. Accelerated replacement demand for old Petrol and Diesel vehicles. Context for why older vehicle registrations decline post-2021.

events.label: Global Financial Crisis — India Credit Tightening
events.detail: Global Financial Crisis impact on India: credit tightening, export demand collapse. All-segment vehicle demand dip Q4 FY09 through Q2 FY10. Pre-Vahan4 era — data quality note: VAHAN data coverage was less complete before 2012.

events.label: Delhi NGT Diesel Ban — Localised Effect
events.detail: National Green Tribunal banned diesel vehicles above 2000cc in Delhi-NCR (Dec 2015, extended). Luxury diesel SUV segment in Delhi effectively killed. Effect localised to NCR; minimal in All India aggregate or AP data.

events.label: RBI Rate Hike Cycle +250bps — 2W Financing Impact
events.detail: RBI raised repo rate by 250bps (May 2022–Feb 2023) to combat inflation. Auto loan EMIs rose 15–20%. Most impacted: 2W Petrol (entry-level, credit-sensitive). 4W also affected but premium segment less so. AP rural 2W shows stronger signal due to higher NBFC dependence.

events.label: Ola/Uber Driver Income Collapse — Cab Demand Cliff
events.detail: Aggregator driver incomes collapsed 2018 as Ola/Uber cut incentives. New driver-partner registrations slowed significantly. Motor Cab (CNG and Diesel) demand dipped after the 2014–2017 surge. Effect visible in Motor Cab class registrations in AP Tier-2 cities (Vizag, Vijayawada).

events.label: Axle Load Norms Relaxed +20% GVW
events.detail: MoRTH relaxed axle load norms by 20% in July 2018, effectively allowing trucks to carry more cargo per trip. Reduced demand for incremental trucks in the short term (existing fleet became more efficient). Context for Diesel CV demand trajectory mid-2018.

events.label: GST Cess on Diesel 4W — 50% Effective Rate
events.detail: GST cess structure: large diesel cars attracted 28% GST + 22% cess = 50% effective rate. This was materially higher than pre-GST incidence. Mid and large diesel SUV segment directly impacted. Reinforces the BS4/GST composite window annotation (A05).

events.label: AP EV Policy 4.0 — 2024–2029
events.detail: Andhra Pradesh launched EV Policy 4.0 (2024–2029) building on 2018–2023 policy. Continued road tax exemption for EVs plus additional incentives for commercial EV operators. Signal visible in AP EV data from 2024 onwards. TDP government maintained and expanded EV incentives.

events.label: PM E-SEWA — EV 3W for Women Micro-Entrepreneurs
events.detail: PM E-SEWA scheme (2024) provided EVs to women micro-entrepreneurs, specifically EV 3-wheelers. Niche segment stimulation — mainly visible in EV 3W category. Small absolute volumes but represents structural government-backed demand.

Maruti Suzuki.label: CNG Wagon R, Alto launched — factory-fitted CNG
Maruti Suzuki.detail: Maruti becomes India's largest CNG seller. Factory-fitted CNG on Wagon R & Alto drives mass adoption.

Maruti Suzuki.label: Strong Hybrid entered (Ciaz hybrid)
Maruti Suzuki.detail: Maruti enters mild/strong hybrid space via Ciaz SHVS — first mainstream hybrid offering at volume prices.

Maruti Suzuki.label: Discontinues Omni, Gypsy — portfolio pruning
Maruti Suzuki.detail: Maruti exits niche utility segments to focus on hatchback/compact SUV core portfolio.

Maruti Suzuki.label: Exits diesel permanently — all models converted to petrol/CNG
Maruti Suzuki.detail: Post BS6, Maruti becomes 100% diesel-free. Diesel volumes collapse from 409K (2017) to near-zero. Structural shift.

Maruti Suzuki.label: Suzuki Gujarat EV battery plant announced
Maruti Suzuki.detail: ₹7,300Cr investment in EV battery manufacturing — signals EV commitment after years of scepticism.

Maruti Suzuki.label: Strong Hybrid re-enters via Grand Vitara
Maruti Suzuki.detail: Strong Hybrid volumes restart with Grand Vitara after 2018 SHVS was limited. Maruti-Toyota tech partnership.

Maruti Suzuki.label: eVX concept revealed at Auto Expo
Maruti Suzuki.detail: First public EV from Maruti — changes investor/consumer perception of EV readiness.

Maruti Suzuki.label: e Vitara EV launch
Maruti Suzuki.detail: Mass-market EV at ₹17–21L. Maruti's volume capability brings EV to mainstream. EV re-entry after 2019 gap.

Tata Motors.label: Tata EV presence via e2o (pre-data era)
Tata Motors.detail: Tata among earliest EV sellers in India. Low volumes but early mover in EV space.

Tata Motors.label: Nexon EV launch ₹13.99L — first mass-market EV
Tata Motors.detail: Nexon EV becomes India's best-selling EV. Positioned at mainstream SUV price — game-changer for EV adoption.

Tata Motors.label: TPG invests $1B in Tata Motors EV subsidiary
Tata Motors.detail: Demerger of PV business + $1B EV investment. Gives Tata dedicated EV funding and faster product cadence.

Tata Motors.label: TML PV demerger into Tata Passenger Vehicles Ltd
Tata Motors.detail: Separate entity for PV allows faster decision-making and EV focus independent of CV business.

Tata Motors.label: Nexon EV Max (40.5 kWh) launched
Tata Motors.detail: Longer range variant addresses range anxiety. Expands Nexon EV addressable market upward.

Tata Motors.label: Punch EV launch — sub-₹10L entry EV
Tata Motors.detail: Punch EV democratises electric mobility. Sub-₹10L starting price opens entirely new buyer segment.

Tata Motors.label: CNG portfolio expansion — Tiago/Tigor/Nexon CNG
Tata Motors.detail: Tata significantly scales CNG offerings in response to CNG demand boom, competing directly with Maruti/Hyundai.

Hyundai.label: CNG Santro, i10 — early CNG presence
Hyundai.detail: Hyundai establishes CNG presence early with Santro and i10 factory CNG. Consistent volumes since 2012.

Hyundai.label: Diesel portfolio peaks — Creta, Verna, Elite i20
Hyundai.detail: Hyundai diesel reaches peak penetration. Creta diesel becomes category-defining SUV in India.

Hyundai.label: Kona EV launch ₹23.7L — premium EV entry
Hyundai.detail: First mainstream EV from Hyundai India. High price limits volumes but establishes EV credibility.

Hyundai.label: CNG re-acceleration — Aura, Grand i10 Nios CNG
Hyundai.detail: Hyundai accelerates CNG range in response to CNG demand surge. Multiple new factory-CNG models.

Hyundai.label: Ioniq 5 India launch — global EV platform
Hyundai.detail: Premium EV at ₹45L. Establishes Hyundai in luxury EV space. Global E-GMP platform localization signal.

Hyundai.label: Creta EV launch — mass-market EV pivot
Hyundai.detail: Creta EV at ₹17–23L: Hyundai's highest-volume model goes electric. Catalyses mainstream EV shift.

Mahindra.label: e2o in market — early urban EV
Mahindra.detail: Mahindra Reva e2o: limited range, urban niche. Early EV volumes small but consistent market presence.

Mahindra.label: Diesel dominance — Scorpio, XUV500, Thar
Mahindra.detail: Mahindra reaches peak diesel volumes. UV portfolio almost entirely diesel — captures rural/semi-urban demand.

Mahindra.label: INGLO EV platform announced — ₹8,000Cr investment
Mahindra.detail: Global EV platform with 5 new EVs planned. Signals complete EV transformation of Mahindra UV lineup.

Mahindra.label: XUV400 EV launch — first Mahindra modern EV
Mahindra.detail: XUV400 EV marks Mahindra's return to serious EV after Reva era. Competitive pricing at ₹15–19L.

Mahindra.label: BE6e / XEV9e launched — new EV architecture
Mahindra.detail: Born-electric SUVs on INGLO platform. Mahindra signals full EV intent for premium UV segment going forward.

Mahindra.label: Petrol portfolio expansion — Thar Roxx 5-door petrol
Mahindra.detail: Mahindra diversifies away from pure diesel with petrol Thar variants. Responds to diesel share decline.

Toyota.label: Diesel dominance — Innova, Fortuner, Etios Diesel
Toyota.detail: Toyota India almost entirely diesel-driven. Innova/Fortuner diesel among India's most durable volume sellers.

Toyota.label: Strong Hybrid pivot — Hyryder, Innova Hycross
Toyota.detail: Toyota enters strong hybrid at scale. Hyryder strong hybrid with Maruti partnership. Innova Hycross replaces Innova Crysta petrol.

Toyota.label: Urban Cruiser Hyryder — Maruti co-development
Toyota.detail: First Toyota-Maruti co-developed product in India. Strong hybrid tech shared at volume prices.

Toyota.label: Innova HyCross strong hybrid launch
Toyota.detail: Replaces diesel Innova Crysta in premium MPV. Strong hybrid captures MPV segment — high average volumes.

Toyota.label: EV portfolio restart — Urban Cruiser EV
Toyota.detail: Toyota re-enters EV after 2019–2024 gap. Leverages Maruti e Vitara platform for India EV market.

Kia.label: Kia Seltos launch — fastest 1L units ever in India
Kia.detail: Kia Seltos: 1L units in 13 months, fastest ever for a new entrant. Petrol and diesel both strong. Redefines mid-SUV.

Kia.label: Kia Sonet — compact SUV segment entry
Kia.detail: Sub-4m SUV at ₹6.7L. Kia captures compact SUV boom. Volume and share doubles in 18 months post-launch.

Kia.label: Kia EV6 — premium EV India launch
Kia.detail: Premium EV at ₹60L. Very low volumes but establishes Kia EV credibility and brand perception.

Kia.label: Carens MPV launch — CNG and diesel
Kia.detail: Kia enters MPV segment with Carens. Multi-fuel (petrol/diesel/CNG) strategy broadens addressable market.

Kia.label: EV9 and Syros announced — EV expansion
Kia.detail: Kia expands EV portfolio downmarket. EV9 premium + Syros sub-₹15L signals mass EV push from 2025.

Volkswagen Group.label: Diesel dominance — Vento, Polo, Jetta diesel
Volkswagen Group.detail: VW Group India built on diesel. Vento diesel and Polo diesel key volume drivers through 2018.

Volkswagen Group.label: EV presence via Audi e-tron (token volumes)
Volkswagen Group.detail: VW Group enters Indian EV via Audi — very small volumes, premium niche only.

Volkswagen Group.label: Taigun and Kushaq India launch — petrol pivot
Volkswagen Group.detail: MQB-A0-IN platform: Taigun (VW) and Kushaq (Skoda) entirely petrol. Marks full exit from diesel for VW Group.

Volkswagen Group.label: CNG exits portfolio — last CNG volume
Volkswagen Group.detail: VW Group exits CNG segment entirely. No CNG models offered after 2023.

Volkswagen Group.label: EV India expansion delayed — global restructuring
Volkswagen Group.detail: VW Group pauses India EV plans amid global financial restructuring. Local EV timeline pushed back.

MG Motor.label: Hector launch — MG India entry
MG Motor.detail: MG Hector marks JSW-SAIC entry into India. Petrol and diesel. 20,000 bookings in hours. Technology-first positioning.

MG Motor.label: ZS EV launch — affordable mass-market EV
MG Motor.detail: ZS EV at ₹21L: one of India's first affordable EVs with real range. Major catalyst for EV consideration in India.

MG Motor.label: CNG entered with Hector Plus
MG Motor.detail: MG offers CNG variant expanding Hector's addressable market to fleet and cost-conscious buyers.

MG Motor.label: JSW Group acquires 35% stake from SAIC
MG Motor.detail: Localisation of ownership changes MG India's India-first strategy. JSW brings supply chain and manufacturing depth.

MG Motor.label: Windsor EV launch — subscription model
MG Motor.detail: Windsor EV with battery-as-a-service. ₹9.99L ex-battery. Disrupts EV ownership model. Becomes top-selling EV.

Honda Cars.label: CNG City, Amaze — strong CNG volumes
Honda Cars.detail: Honda CNG City and Amaze become consistent sellers. CNG volumes grow steadily through 2019.

Honda Cars.label: Diesel portfolio peaks — Amaze, City, Jazz, WR-V diesel
Honda Cars.detail: Honda diesel reaches peak. City diesel and Amaze diesel key volume drivers in mid sedan/compact segment.

Honda Cars.label: Diesel volumes collapse — BS6 transition shock
Honda Cars.detail: Honda diesel volumes fall sharply in BS6 transition. Jazz, WR-V diesel never recover commercial viability.

Honda Cars.label: Jazz, WR-V, Civic discontinued — portfolio pruned
Honda Cars.detail: Honda exits Jazz, WR-V and Civic India. Portfolio narrows to City, Amaze and City hybrid. Diesel effectively ends.

Honda Cars.label: City e:HEV strong hybrid launch
Honda Cars.detail: Honda enters strong hybrid with City e:HEV. Premium positioning at ₹19L. Full hybrid, not mild.

Honda Cars.label: Elevate SUV launch — petrol/CNG focus
Honda Cars.detail: Honda's first India SUV. Petrol and CNG only — no diesel. Signals Honda's post-diesel India strategy.

Renault.label: CNG Fluence, Scala — early CNG presence
Renault.detail: Renault offers CNG on Fluence/Scala — small niche volumes. Not a significant revenue driver.

Renault.label: Kwid launch — petrol-only mass market entry
Renault.detail: Kwid at ₹2.57L: disrupts entry hatchback segment. Petrol only, high fuel efficiency. Renault's volume backbone.

Renault.label: EV portfolio tested — Zoe imported
Renault.detail: Renault evaluates India EV market with Zoe. Token volumes. Decision made to wait for local production cost parity.

Renault.label: Kiger launch — compact SUV entry
Renault.detail: Sub-4m SUV at ₹5.45L. Kiger and Triber extend Renault's affordable petrol/CNG lineup. Volume recovery.

Renault.label: EV restart — import-led
Renault.detail: Renault re-enters EV space via imports. Low volumes but signals preparation for future local EV production.

Renault.label: Strong Hybrid entry — Renault Symbioz
Renault.detail: Renault enters strong hybrid segment for the first time in India. Leverages Alliance technology with Nissan.
